Date: 2026-05-10
Status: PASS for Slice 1 gated kernel-side cgroup allowlist filtering.

## Scope verified

This slice adds an optional, disabled-by-default cgroup allowlist gate to the
existing process lifecycle eBPF smoke path.

Implemented surface:

- `go/pkg/kernelcapture/process_exec.bpf.c` declares:
  - `filter_control`, a one-entry control map for enabling/disabling the filter.
  - `allowed_cgroups`, a bounded cgroup-id allowlist map.
  - a pre-ring-buffer cgroup check in both exec and exit handlers.
- `go/pkg/kernelcapture/linux_ebpf_smoke_linux.go` adds Go helpers to enable the
  filter, update/delete allowlist entries, read the current unified cgroup id,
  and run positive/negative live proof helpers.
- `go/pkg/kernelcapture/linux_ebpf_smoke_linux_test.go` adds gated privileged
  positive and negative smoke tests behind `ARDUR_RUN_EBPF_SMOKE=1`.
- Generated eBPF artifacts were refreshed from the Linux dev image:
  - `go/pkg/kernelcapture/processexec_bpfel.go`
  - `go/pkg/kernelcapture/processexec_bpfel.o`

## Verification commands

```bash
cd go && GOCACHE=/tmp/ardur-go-build-cache go test ./pkg/kernelcapture -count=1
cd go && GOCACHE=/tmp/ardur-go-build-cache go test -race ./pkg/kernelcapture -count=1
cd go && GOCACHE=/tmp/ardur-go-build-cache GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go test ./pkg/kernelcapture -c -o /tmp/kernelcapture-linux-arm64.test
./scripts/run-phase2-ebpf-smoke.sh
```

All commands passed after regenerating the eBPF artifacts in the documented Linux
Podman dev image.

## Generated artifact reproducibility

The generated eBPF artifacts were regenerated twice from the current
`process_exec.bpf.c` source in the Linux dev image. A repeat generation produced
identical hashes:

```text
04b78ae5a82afedfa573860313871533084ea2c74d665e998732f2d2b40e  go/pkg/kernelcapture/processexec_bpfel.go
8e61a76292729f46c0a6c3c6be35129344d684ab827b784842c63c768a5ccf22  go/pkg/kernelcapture/processexec_bpfel.o
```

The generated Go bindings expose the new `allowed_cgroups` and `filter_control`
maps, and the repeat-generation hash proof shows the checked-in generated
artifacts are stable for this source/toolchain combination.

## Live privileged Linux smoke result

The rootful smoke run exercised the existing exec/session tests plus the new
positive and negative cgroup-filter tests:

```text
=== RUN   TestLinuxEBPFExecSmoke
--- PASS: TestLinuxEBPFExecSmoke
=== RUN   TestLinuxEBPFSessionSmoke
--- PASS: TestLinuxEBPFSessionSmoke
=== RUN   TestLinuxEBPFCgroupFilterPositiveSmoke
    filter_enabled=true allowed_cgroup=25130 target_pid=40211 exec_cgroup=25130 exit_cgroup=25130
--- PASS: TestLinuxEBPFCgroupFilterPositiveSmoke
=== RUN   TestLinuxEBPFCgroupFilterNegativeSmoke
    filter_enabled=true blocked_cgroup=25130 target_pid=40212 negative_timed_out=true
--- PASS: TestLinuxEBPFCgroupFilterNegativeSmoke
PASS
```

Full raw output: `reports/phase2-ebpf-smoke-output-rootful.txt`.

## Important limitations

This is a narrow kernel-capture MVP proof, not a complete daemon feature yet.

- The filter is not wired into daemon custody or mission configuration yet.
- The smoke harness uses the current test-runner cgroup; it does not create or
  manage per-session cgroups.
- The negative test proves that events from the current cgroup are not emitted
  after the current cgroup is absent from `allowed_cgroups`; it does not prove a
  full multi-tenant daemon admission model.
- File/network capture remains future work.

## Residual risk

The implementation has live kernel proof on the documented Linux dev image and a
repeat-generation hash proof for the generated eBPF artifacts. Remaining risk is
product-integration risk, not Slice 1 proof failure: the filter still needs daemon
custody wiring, mission-config mapping, and operational sequencing before it can
be described as production daemon enforcement.
